title: JavaScript Closures Explained Simply
short_description: Closures ek powerful concept hai JavaScript mein. Is article mein hum simple examples se samjhenge ki closures kaise kaam karte hain.
full_content:
Closure kya hota hai?

Jab ek function apne outer scope ke variables ko yaad rakhta hai even after outer function execute ho chuka ho, use closure kehte hain.

function counter() {
  let count = 0;
  return function() {
    count++;
    return count;
  };
}
const inc = counter();
console.log(inc()); // 1
console.log(inc()); // 2

Yahan inner function count variable ko closure ke through access karta hai.

title: CSS Grid vs Flexbox — Kab Kya Use Karein?
short_description: CSS Grid aur Flexbox dono powerful layout tools hain. Lekin dono ka use case alag hai — aao samjhein kab kaunsa use karna chahiye.
full_content:
Flexbox — 1D Layout

Flexbox ek direction mein kaam karta hai — ya toh row ya column. Jab tumhe items ko ek line mein align karna ho, Flexbox best hai.

.container {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: center;
}

Grid — 2D Layout

Grid rows aur columns dono mein kaam karta hai. Complex page layouts ke liye Grid use karo.

.container {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
  gap: 1rem;
}

title: Python List Comprehension — Clean Code Likhne Ka Tarika
short_description: List comprehension Python ka ek elegant feature hai jo loops ko ek line mein likhne deta hai. Isse code readable aur fast banta hai.
full_content:
Normal Loop vs List Comprehension

Pehle normal loop:

squares = []
for i in range(10):
    squares.append(i**2)

Ab list comprehension se:

squares = [i**2 for i in range(10)]

Condition ke saath

evens = [i for i in range(20) if i % 2 == 0]

Yeh sirf even numbers ki list banata hai.
